name: crease.works
description: a platform for facilitators who want to take play seriously. design playful learning experiences, run them with real groups, and collect evidence of what actually happens — not just what you hoped would happen. iterate your designs based on what children and participants really do when they play.
features: co-play reflection for facilitators and participants
iteration workflows that learn from what happened

name: vertigo.vault
description: a growing library of group activities, ice-breakers, and energisers — gathered from years of facilitation across cultures, classrooms, and community spaces. find the right game for any group size, energy level, or moment. every activity has been tested with real humans in real rooms.
features: searchable by group size, energy, and purpose
field-tested across cultures and age groups
no signup required — just browse and play

name: deep.deck
description: a card game for teachers and parents who want to actually connect with children ages 6–14. layered conversation prompts move from light to deep, wild-card modifiers lower the stakes of vulnerable questions, and playful mini-games keep everyone in the room. designed so the hard conversations happen sideways.
features: wild cards that make hard questions feel safe
